steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Recognize two pairs of corresponding angles are already equal
steps.content: We are given $\angle D = \angle R = 47^{\circ}$ and $\angle E = \angle S = 97^{\circ}$. Under the natural correspondence ($D \leftrightarrow R$, $E \leftrightarrow S$), we already have two pairs of corresponding angles that are congruent.

steps.title: Apply AA similarity criterion
steps.content:
The AA (Angle-Angle) criterion for similarity states: if two pairs of corresponding angles are congruent, the triangles are similar.

We already have this condition satisfied: $\angle D = \angle R$ and $\angle E = \angle S$.

Therefore, $\triangle DEF \sim \triangle RST$ without needing to know the measures of angles $F$ and $T$.

That confirms D is correct.
